lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Hello mortal. Have you come to retrieve something you lost when you died?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does that work?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: When you die, you often drop things you were carrying. In most cases, a gravestone appears near where you died, and your items can be retrieved there.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: However, gravestones do not last for long. When your gravestone collapses, your items come here, to me. I can return them to you - though I charge a fee for my services.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Aside from that, if you bring me your unwanted possessions, and sacrifice them into my Coffer here, I'll count their value against any future fees I charge you.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: I don't accept just any junk, but I'll try to give you a good price for anything decent.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: So do you need to retrieve something you lost when you died?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: As you should be able to see, I am Death. The Grim Reaper. The Inexorable One. The Inescapable Grasp of Mortal Destiny. And this is my little office. Nice, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's lovely.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Thank you. I feel the colour scheme suits my style. Now, can I help you retrieve something you lost when you died?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your decorations leave a bit to be desired.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Sometimes it's hard to care for beauty when one's very existence is just seen as a curse upon the world. Now, do you actually want to retrieve something from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, have you got anything for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I choose a different-looking gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You haven't got 200,000 coins to pay for that. I can take coins from your inventory, or from my Coffer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I choose a different-looking gravestone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Most people won't see your grave, by the way. It appears to you, but it aims to be invisible to everyone else. Are you sure you want to buy a cosmetic override for it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I understand other people won't often see it.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: It is not for me to judge how people spend their money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, it's not worth it.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: It is not for me to judge how people spend their money.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Do you really want to switch to the basic gravestone? I'm not offering to give back the fee you paid earlier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want the basic gravestone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You've already got the basic gravestone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I choose a different-looking gravestone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Er... I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: How would you like to... die?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Really? There isn't much else for you to do here. But you know your business.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Hello mortal. If you're looking for something you lost when you died, I'm afraid I don't cater to Ultimate Ironmen. Your items would have fallen on the ground where you died.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I change how I die?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Er... I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: How would you like to... die?

sections.title: Upon losing any items while dying for the first time
Talking to Death again after reaching the dialogue options for the first time but before completeing the tutorial

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: I believe I was explaining gravestones to you. We still have some more topics to cover.

sections.title: Attempting to leave before finishing the tutorial

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: I haven't finished talking to you yet, [player name]. Please don't worry about your gravestone - it will wait until you leave this place.

sections.title: Talking to Death after completing the tutorial, but before leaving Death's Office

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Would you like me to remind you about gravestones?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: So, mortal... you have died, as all mortals do. As you have suffered the loss of some items on this occasion, I have brought you here to explain for you how that works.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost items? Can I get them back?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Let me explain... Across most of the land of Gielinor, when you die, some of the items you're carrying are dropped.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: I will look after such items, without a time limit. However, I do charge more for my services. It would be cheaper to retrieve them from the gravestone directly, if you can reach it in time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about gravestones again.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Gladly... Across most of the land of Gielinor, when you die, some of the items you're carrying are dropped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I pay a gravestone fee?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Cash is always acceptable. Also, a gravestone can take it directly from you bank, in case you didn't have enough with you.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Besides that, take a look at my Coffer here. You can bring your unwanted possessions, and sacrifice them into my Coffer. I'll count their value against any future gravestone fees you're charged.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long do I have to return to my gravestone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I know what will happen to my items when I die?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: There is a menu to predict these things for you. Take a look at your Worn Items side-panel...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm done here.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: I'm afraid we have more topics to cover before you leave. Speak to me when you are ready to continue.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Very well. Once you pass through my portal, you will have 15 minutes to reach your gravestone.

sections.title: Choosing how you would like to die

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: How would you like to... die?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You haven't unlocked that. You can do so by reading a Trailblazer reloaded death scroll.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You will now die like a Trailblazer.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You are already using that.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You are already using that.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: You will now perish like most others in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Hello mortal. If you're looking for something you lost when you died, I'm afraid I don't cater to Ultimate Iron [Men/Women]. Your items would have fallen on the ground where you died.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can I do here?

lines.speaker: Death
lines.text: Nothing, really. Please leave when you are ready.
